title: Almond flourless cake
subtitle: A dense, fudgy chocolate almond cake with no flour
excerpt: A naturally gluten-free cake built on almond flour, dark chocolate, and eggs. Dense, fudgy, and deeply chocolatey, it improves overnight as the texture firms and the flavour deepens.
sourceNotes: The flourless chocolate cake is embedded in both French and Italian pastry traditions (torta caprese from Capri is among the most documented versions). The technique of folding beaten eggs into melted chocolate produces the characteristic fudgy centre and slightly sunken top. Maida Heatter's Book of Great Chocolate Desserts (1980) and Nigella Lawson's How to Be a Domestic Goddess (2000) both document versions central to the home-baking canon.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely. Wrap the whole cake tightly in cling film and freeze for up to 2 months. Thaw overnight in the fridge.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The cake is better the day after baking. Store at room temperature, covered, for up to 3 days.

baking.decoratingTechnique: dusted with icing sugar before serving

glossaryTerms.definition: A method of melting chocolate gently by placing it in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water, without the bowl touching the water. The indirect heat prevents the chocolate scorching or seizing.

content.text: Without flour to hold it up, this cake rises during baking and sinks as it cools. That is not a fault. The slight dip in the centre is where the texture is most fudgy, and the cracked surface is characteristic. Do not attempt to level it.
Do not overbake. The cake is done when the edges are set but the centre still has a slight wobble. It will firm up as it cools. An overbaked flourless cake becomes dry and chalky rather than fudgy.
Melt the chocolate and mix the base
Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Grease and line a 20 cm round cake tin. Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} and {{unsalted-butter}} together using a
, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat and stir in 100 g of the {{caster-sugar}}, the egg yolks, {{vanilla-extract}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}}. Fold in the {{almond-flour}} and {{cocoa-powder}}.
Fold in the egg whites and bake, 28–32 minutes

In a clean bowl, beat the egg whites with a hand mixer until soft peaks form. Add the remaining 50 g of {{caster-sugar}} and continue beating until firm, glossy peaks hold. Fold one-third of the egg whites into the chocolate mixture to loosen it, then fold in the remaining two-thirds in two additions, cutting through the batter rather than stirring, until just combined. Pour into the prepared tin and bake for 28 to 32 minutes. The edges should be set and the centre should have a slight wobble. Cool in the tin for 30 minutes before turning out. Dust with {{icing-sugar}} to serve.
